markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Guiding Finish](/Feats.aspx?ID=416) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fighter" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=71" />
<trait label="Press" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=133" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 152

**Requirements**
You are wielding only a single one-handed melee weapon and have your other hand or hands free.

</column>

---

Using your weapon as a lever, you force your opponent to end up right where you want them. Make a Strike with the required weapon. If the Strike hits, you can move the target up to 10 feet into a space in your reach. You can move the target through your space during this movement. This follows the [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) rules. Your Strike gains the following failure effect.

**Failure** You can force the creature to move as you would on a success, but you can move the target only 5 feet.
name: Guiding Finish
requirement_markdown: You are wielding only a single one-handed melee weapon and have your other hand or hands free.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crane Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=430) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=152" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 158

**Archetypes** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67) (Level 4), [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226) (Level 4), [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=255) (Level 4)

**Requirements**
You are unarmored.

</column>

---

You enter the stance of a crane, holding your arms in an imitation of a crane’s wings and using flowing, defensive motions. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC, but the only Strikes you can make are crane wing attacks. These deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage; are in the brawling group; and have the agile, finesse, nonlethal, and unarmed traits.

 While in Crane Stance, reduce the DC for High Jump and Long Jump by 5, and when you Leap, you can move an additional 5 feet horizontally or 2 feet vertically.

<title level="2">Crane Stance leads to...</title>
[Crane Flutter](/Feats.aspx?ID=450)
name: Crane Stance
requirement_markdown: You are unarmored.

>
[Monastic Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=434) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 158

</column>

---

You have trained with the traditional weaponry of your monastery or school. You gain access to uncommon weapons that have the [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=112) trait and become trained in simple and martial monk weapons. When your proficiency rank for unarmed attacks increases to expert or master, your proficiency rank for these weapons increases to expert or master as well.

 You can use melee monk weapons with any of your monk feats or monk abilities that normally require unarmed attacks, though not if the feat or ability requires you to use a single specific type of attack, such as [Crane Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=430).

<title level="2">Monastic Weaponry leads to...</title>
[Ancestral Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=1736), [Peafowl Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1739), [Shooting Stars Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1737), [Whirling Blade Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1755)

<traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mountain Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=435) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=152" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 159

**Archetypes** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67) (Level 4), [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=255) (Level 4)

**Requirements**
You are unarmored and touching the ground.

</column>

---

You enter the stance of an implacable mountain—a technique first discovered by dwarven monks—allowing you to strike with the weight of an avalanche. The only Strikes you can make are falling stone unarmed attacks. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

 While in Mountain Stance, you gain a +4 item bonus to AC and a +2 circumstance bonus to any defenses against being [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Tripped](/Actions.aspx?ID=40). However, you have a Dexterity modifier cap to your AC of +0, meaning you don't add your Dexterity to your AC, and your Speeds are all reduced by 5 feet. The item bonus to AC from Mountain Stance is cumulative with [_armor potency runes_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278) on your [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2), [_mage armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=176), and [_bracers of armor_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416).

<title level="2">Mountain Stance leads to...</title>
[Mountain Stronghold](/Feats.aspx?ID=453)
name: Mountain Stance

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 162 **Archetypes** [Wrestler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=127) (Level 8), [Wrestler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=270) (Level 8) **Requirements** You have a creature grabbed or restrained. --- You propel your grabbed or restrained foe a great distance. You can throw the creature any distance up to 10 feet, plus 5 feet × your Strength modifier. If you successfully throw the creature, it takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier plus 1d6 per 10 feet you threw it. Attempt an Athletics check against the foe’s Fortitude DC. You take a –2 circumstance penalty to your check if the target is one size larger than you and a –4 circumstance penalty if it’s larger than that. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check if the target is one size smaller than you and a +4 circumstance bonus if it’s smaller than that. **Critical Success** You throw the creature the desired distance and it lands prone.
**Success** You throw the creature the desired distance.
**Failure** You don’t throw the creature.
**Critical Failure** You don’t throw the creature, and it’s no longer grabbed or restrained by you.
name: Whirling Throw
requirement_markdown: You have a creature grabbed or restrained.

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 171 **Archetypes** [Steel Falcon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=374) (Level 6), [Nidalese Horselord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=381) (Level 4) --- You have studied a specific terrain to overcome its challenges. Choose aquatic, arctic, desert, forest, mountain, plains, sky, swamp, or underground as your favored terrain. When in that terrain, you can ignore the effects of non-magical diffcult terrain. If you have the wild stride class feature, you gain a second benefit while in your favored terrain, depending on your choice.
  • **Aquatic** You gain a swim Speed equal to your Speed. If you already had a swim Speed, you gain a +10-foot status bonus to your swim Speed.
  • **Arctic** You need to eat and drink only one-tenth as much as usual, you aren’t affected by severe or extreme cold, and you can walk across ice and snow at full Speed without needing to Balance.
  • **Desert** You need to eat and drink only one-tenth as much as usual, you aren’t affected by severe or extreme heat, and you can walk along sand at full Speed without needing to Balance.
  • **Forest, Mountain, or Underground** You gain a climb Speed equal to your Speed. If you already had a climb Speed, you gain a +10-foot status bonus to your climb Speed.
  • **Plains** You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your land Speed.
  • **Sky** You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your fly Speed, if you have one.
  • **Swamp** You can move across bogs at full Speed, even if they are deep enough to be greater difficult terrain or to normally require you to Swim.
Favored Terrain leads to... [Terrain Master](/Feats.aspx?ID=517)
name: Favored Terrain

Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); [Snare Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=843)

</column>

---

You specialize in creating quick traps to obstruct your enemies on the battlefield. If your proficiency rank in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) is expert, you gain the formulas for three common or [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) [snares](/Equipment.aspx?Category=31). If your rank is master, you gain 6. If your rank is legendary, you gain 9.

 Each day during your daily preparations, you can prepare four snares from your formula book for quick deployment; if they normally take 1 minute to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43), you can Craft them with 3 Interact actions. The number of snares increases to six if you have master proficiency in Crafting and eight if you have legendary proficiency in Crafting. Snares prepared in this way don't cost you any resources to Craft.

<title level="2">Snare Specialist leads to...</title>
[Lightning Snares](/Feats.aspx?ID=527), [Powerful Snares](/Feats.aspx?ID=516), [Quick Snares](/Feats.aspx?ID=509), [Trapsmith Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3134), [Trapsmith Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8636), [Ubiquitous Snares](/Feats.aspx?ID=539)

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 184 --- With careful observation during battle, you identify an enemy’s strengths and weaknesses. The GM rolls a secret Perception check for you against the Deception or Stealth DC (whichever is higher) of an enemy of your choice who is not concealed from you, hidden from you, or undetected by you, and who is engaged in combat. The GM might apply a penalty for the distance between you and the enemy. The enemy is then temporarily immune to your Battle Assessment for 1 day. **Critical Success** The GM chooses two of the following pieces of information about the enemy to tell you: which of the enemy’s weaknesses is highest, which of the enemy’s saving throws has the lowest modifier, one immunity the enemy has, or which of the enemy’s resistances is highest. If the event of a tie, the GM should pick one at random.
**Success** The GM chooses one piece of information from the above list to tell you about the enemy.
**Critical Failure** The GM gives you false information (the GM makes up the information).
name: Battle Assessment

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Investigator" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=318" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 65, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 189

**Trigger**
You specify the trigger when you make your daily preparations (see Requirements below).

**Requirements**
When you make your daily preparations, you must specify a trigger for this reaction using the same restrictions as the triggers for the Ready action. You also choose a single spell from the arcane, divine, occult, or primal list of 4th level or lower. The spell can’t have a cost, nor can its casting time be more than 10 minutes. The spell must be able to target a single creature, and you must be a valid target for it.

</column>

---

Whether from jury-rigged magic items, stolen magical essence, or other means, you have a contingency in your back pocket for desperate situations. When the trigger occurs, you cause the spell to come into effect. The spell targets only you, no matter how many creatures it would affect normally. If you define particularly complicated conditions, as determined by the GM, the trigger might fail. Once the contingency is triggered, the spell is expended until your next daily preparations.
name: Trickster's Ace

The spell must be able to target a single creature, and you must be a valid target for it.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=633) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Sorcerer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=148" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=323" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 100, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 209, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 198

**Trigger**
A creature Casts a Spell that you have prepared.

**Requirements**
[Sorcerer] You have an unexpended spell slot you could use to cast the triggering spell.

</column>

---

When a foe Casts a Spell and you can see its manifestations, you can use your own magic to counter it.

**[Witch, Wizard]** You expend a prepared spell to counter the triggering creature’s casting of that same spell. You lose your spell slot as if you had cast the triggering spell. You then attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell.

**[Sorcerer]** You expend one of your spell slots to counter the triggering creature’s casting of a spell that you have in your repertoire. You lose your spell slot as if you had cast the triggering spell. You then attempt to counteract the triggering spell.

**Special** [Sorcerer] This feat has the trait corresponding to the tradition of spells you cast (arcane, divine, occult, or primal).

<title level="2">Counterspell leads to...</title>
[Clever Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=653), [Consume Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1827), [Reflect Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=624), [School Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=3026), [School Spell Redirection](/Feats.aspx?ID=3027)
name: Counterspell
requirement_markdown: [Sorcerer] You have an unexpended spell slot you could use to cast the triggering spell.
